AccessControlBadConfirmation().details: The caller of a function is not the expected one. NOTE: Don't confuse with {AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount}.

AddressEmptyCode(address).details: There's no code at `target` (it is not a contract).

ERC1967InvalidImplementation(address).details: The `implementation` of the proxy is invalid.

ERC1967NonPayable().details: An upgrade function sees `msg.value > 0` that may be lost.

FailedCall().details: A call to an address target failed. The target may have reverted.

SafeERC20FailedOperation(address).details: An operation with an ERC-20 token failed.

UUPSUnauthorizedCallContext().details: The call is from an unauthorized context.

UUPSUnsupportedProxiableUUID(bytes32).details: The storage `slot` is unsupported as a UUID.

Initialized(uint64).details: Triggered when the contract has been initialized or reinitialized.

RoleAdminChanged(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32.details: Emitted when `newAdminRole` is set as ``role``'s admin role, replacing `previousAdminRole` `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is the starting admin for all roles, despite {RoleAdminChanged} not being emitted to signal this.

RoleGranted(bytes32,address,address).details: Emitted when `account` is granted `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call. This account bears the admin role (for the granted role). Expected in cases where the role was granted using the internal {AccessControl-_grantRole}.

RoleRevoked(bytes32,address,address).details: Emitted when `account` is revoked `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call:   - if using `revokeRole`, it is the admin role bearer   - if using `renounceRole`, it is the role bearer (i.e. `account`)

Upgraded(address).details: Emitted when the implementation is upgraded.

cancelDeposit(uint256).details: Refund amounts (net + fee) are added to depositEscrowBalance for the sender to claim later      via claimDepositEscrow().

params._len: Number of requests to cancel (0 = cancel all)

cancelPendingRedeem(uint256).details: User specified they CANNOT cancel pending redeems - only MAINTAINER can do emergency cancellations      No token fees taken (fees are in redeemAsset).      Restores offchainShares and totalRedeemQueueTokens (reverse of requestRedeem).

params._len: Number of pending requests to cancel

cancelRedeem(uint256).details: Refunds full amount - no token fees taken (fees are in redeemAsset).      Restores offchainShares and totalRedeemQueueTokens (reverse of requestRedeem).

circulatingSupply().details: Circulating = Total Supply - Tokens committed to redeem queues (pending + final) - Live mgt fee tokens.      totalRedeemQueueTokens is incremented at requestRedeem time, covering both pending and final queues.

claimDepositEscrow(address,address).details: Deposit assets are escrowed when cancelDeposit() cannot transfer back to the sender (e.g. sender was banned)Can be called by the escrowed user directly or by an operator on their behalf

params._account: Address to claim escrow for (ignored when caller is not OPERATOR_ROLE, uses msg.sender)
params._asset: Address of the deposit asset to claim

claimRedeemEscrow(address).details: Tokens are escrowed when cancel() cannot transfer back to the sender (e.g. sender was banned)Can be called by the escrowed user directly or by an operator on their behalf

params._account: Address to claim escrow for (ignored when caller is not OPERATOR_ROLE, uses msg.sender)

returns.shareAmount: Offchain share amount (ratio baked in at request time)
Offchain share amount (ratio baked in at request time)

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

getRoleMember(bytes32,uint256).details: Returns one of the accounts that have `role`. `index` must be a value between 0 and {getRoleMemberCount}, non-inclusive. Role bearers are not sorted in any particular way, and their ordering may change at any point. WARNING: When using {getRoleMember} and {getRoleMemberCount}, make sure you perform all queries on the same block. See the following https://forum.openzeppelin.com/t/iterating-over-elements-on-enumerableset-in-openzeppelin-contracts/2296[forum post] for more information.

getRoleMemberCount(bytes32).details: Returns the number of accounts that have `role`. Can be used together with {getRoleMember} to enumerate all bearers of a role.

getRoleMembers(bytes32).details: Return all accounts that have `role` WARNING: This operation will copy the entire storage to memory, which can be quite expensive. This is designed to mostly be used by view accessors that are queried without any gas fees. Developers should keep in mind that this function has an unbounded cost, and using it as part of a state-changing function may render the function uncallable if the set grows to a point where copying to memory consumes too much gas to fit in a block.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

params._depositMaxDeviationBps: Symmetric oracle deviation tolerance for processDepositQueue (BPS)
params._kycManager: Address of the KYC manager (required, non-zero; shared with Token)
params._maxStalePeriod: Maximum staleness in seconds (e.g., 86400 for 24 hours)
params._priceOracle: Address of the price oracle (can be zero address if not using oracle)
params._redeemAsset: Address of the redeem asset (e.g., USDC, USDO)
params._redeemMaxDeviationBps: Symmetric oracle deviation tolerance for processPendingRedeems (BPS)

params._amount: Amount of redeem asset to transfer

params._asset: The asset token address (e.g., USDC, USDO)

returns.netMintAmt: Net token amount to be minted after fees

params._tokenAmount: The amount of tokens to redeem

returns.netRedeemAssetAmt: Net redeemAsset amount after fee deduction
returns.redeemAssetAmt: Gross redeemAsset amount queued before fee deduction

processDepositQueue(uint256,uint256).details: Two-pass algorithm: first pass peeks entries to sum normalized net assets and validate KYC,      then sanity-checks operator-supplied _newShares against the oracle and computes total mint.      Second pass pops and mints pro-rata. This preserves the sharesPerToken ratio exactly      (see design spec for invariance proof).

params._len: Number of requests to process (0 = process all)
params._newShares: Actual offchain fund shares acquired for this batch (must be >0 when _len >0)

processPendingRedeems(uint256,uint256).details: Two-pass: Pass 1 pops ready entries, validates KYC, sums shareAmounts, and (if oracle      configured) accumulates oracle-derived expected total. Then deviation-checks      _totalAsset against expectedTotal. Pass 2 distributes _totalAsset pro-rata by      shareAmount and computes per-entry fee on the operator-derived slice.      Stops at the first not-ready entry. If no entries are ready, reverts      NoPendingRedeemsReady. _totalAsset is interpreted relative to the entries actually      processed (not the requested _len).

params._len: Number of pending requests to attempt (must be > 0)
params._totalAsset: Operator-supplied actual redeem asset pool to distribute pro-rata

processRedeemQueue(uint256).details: Burns full token amount, splits redeemAsset between user and txFeeTo.      Decrements totalRedeemQueueTokens (token supply decreases by same amount via burn,      keeping the sharesPerToken ratio invariant).

params._len: Number of requests to process (0 = process all)

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

requestDeposit(address,uint256,address).details: Fee is charged at request time and net assets are forwarded to treasury

params._asset: The asset token address (e.g., USDC, USDO)
params._receiver: Address that receives minted token on processing

requestDirectRedeem(address,uint256,addr.details: Burns tokens immediately. The redeem-asset payout is handled fully off-chain;      the contract only emits the burn record for the DB to match against.      No queue, no T+2 delay; fees are applied off-chain at settlement time.      Accounting: decrements offchainShares by the share-equivalent at current ratio.      Does NOT touch totalRedeemQueueTokens (no in-flight tokens — burn is immediate).      _sharesPerToken stays invariant: numerator drops by shareAmount, denominator      drops by _tokenAmount (totalSupply burn), and shareAmount = _tokenAmount * ratio.      The mgtFeeTo wallet is rejected upfront — fee shares must redeem through      requestRedeem only, to keep totalMgtFeeUnclaimed reconciliation clean.

params._asset: Informational asset address the user wants to receive off-chain               (e.g. RLUSD). Must be non-zero and not equal to redeemAsset.
params._to: KYC'd recipient address recorded for off-chain settlement.

requestRedeem(address,uint256).details: Request goes to pending queue. offchainShares is decremented immediately to maintain      sharesPerToken invariance. The shareAmount is encoded in the queue entry for      later use in processPendingRedeems (no snapshot needed).      For the mgtFeeTo wallet, the caller-supplied _tokenAmount is IGNORED and      the full live fee balance (totalMgtFeeUnclaimed) is redeemed instead.      totalMgtFeeUnclaimed is zeroed at request time.

params._to: The address to receive redeemed asset
params._tokenAmount: The amount of tokens to redeem (IGNORED if caller is mgtFeeTo)

revertRedeemToPending(uint256).details: Used when wrong T+2 price was applied - moves items back to pending for repricing.      Simplified: no snapshot migration needed (ratio baked into queue entry at request time).      totalRedeemQueueTokens and offchainShares are NOT modified — they were adjusted at      requestRedeem time and cover both pending and final queue entries.

params._len: Number of redeems to revert (0 = revert all)

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

setKycManager(address).details: Queue-empty guard: queue processors re-check KYC, so rotating mid-flight could      strand entries whose user is KYC'd in the old manager but not the new.

sharesPerToken().details: Shares per Token = Circulating Supply / Total Supply (scaled by 1e18)

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

params._amount: The amount to calculate fee for

updateConvertRedeemRequestsDelay(uint256.details: Can be adjusted for holidays (e.g., 2 days → 3 days → back to 2 days).      OPERATIONAL INVARIANT (not enforced on-chain to save gas / bytecode):      do NOT call this while pendingRedeemQueue or redeemQueue is non-empty — changing      the delay retroactively shifts the settlement timing of already-queued entries      relative to what the user saw when they submitted. Maintainers should drain both      redeem queues before adjusting this value.

params._delay: Delay in seconds before pending requests can be processed

params._rate: The new fee rate in basis points

params._bps: Deviation tolerance in basis points (0 = strict, max = BPS_BASE)

updateEpoch().details: Fee is charged on the offchain BNY value (the real AUM), not on circulating HYBOND.      This matches how a real-world fund charges mgmt fees on AUM rather than on issued shares.      Can only be called after timeBuffer has elapsed since last update.

params._maxStalePeriod: Maximum staleness in seconds (e.g., 86400 for 24 hours)

params._rate: The new management fee rate in basis points (e.g., 300 for 3% annually)

updateMgtFeeTo(address).details: Requires all live fee tokens to be redeemed and burned first (totalMgtFeeUnclaimed == 0).      Also requires both redeem queues (pendingRedeemQueue, redeemQueue) to be empty.      In-flight entries from the old mgtFeeTo would carry its address as `from`; on      cancel/revert those branches would fail to match the new mgtFeeTo and skip the      totalMgtFeeUnclaimed decrement, corrupting the live fee tracker.

params._address: The new management fee recipient address

updateOffchainShares(uint256).details: Should almost never be called. Use only for error correction or corporate actions.      offchainShares is normally maintained automatically by processDepositQueue (increment)      and requestRedeem (decrement).

updateRedeemAsset(address).details: Requires all queues (deposit, pendingRedeem, redeem) to be empty to prevent      in-flight requests from being settled in the wrong asset.      Claim escrow mappings (redeemEscrowBalance, depositEscrowBalance) are unaffected      because redeemEscrowBalance holds HYBOND tokens (not redeemAsset) and      depositEscrowBalance is keyed by the original deposit asset.

params._rate: The new fee rate in basis points

params._bps: Deviation tolerance in basis points (0 = strict, max = BPS_BASE)

params._timeBuffer: Time buffer in seconds (e.g., 72000 for 20 hours)

params._address: The new transaction fee recipient address

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

__gap.details: Storage gap for future upgrades See https://docs.openzeppelin.com/contracts/4.x/upgradeable#storage_gaps

cancelDeposit(uint256).notice: Cancel queued deposit requests and credit refunds to deposit escrow

cancelPendingRedeem(uint256).notice: Cancel pending redeem requests and refund full token amount

cancelRedeem(uint256).notice: Cancel redeem requests and refund full token amount

claimDepositEscrow(address,address).notice: Claim escrowed deposit assets from cancelled deposits that could not be refunded directly

claimRedeemEscrow(address).notice: Claim escrowed tokens from cancelled redemptions that could not be refunded directly

convertFromUnderlying(address,uint256).notice: Convert asset token amount to token amount

convertToUnderlying(address,uint256).notice: Convert token amount to asset token amount

getDepositQueueInfo(uint256).notice: Get deposit queue info at index

getPendingRedeemQueueInfo(uint256).notice: Get pending redeem queue info at index

getPrice().notice: Get fresh price from price feed with staleness check

getRedeemQueueInfo(uint256).notice: Get redeem queue information at index

getTokenBalance(address).notice: Get token balance held by this contract

kycManager().notice: KYC manager (single source of truth, shared with Token). Required (non-zero).

offRamp(uint256).notice: Transfer redeem asset from contract to treasury

processDepositQueue(uint256,uint256).notice: Process queued deposit requests with pro-rata minting

processPendingRedeems(uint256,uint256).notice: Process pending redeems with operator-supplied total asset distributed pro-rata

processRedeemQueue(uint256).notice: Process redeem queue with pre-calculated amounts

requestDeposit(address,uint256,address).notice: Queue a deposit request by depositing asset

requestDirectRedeem(address,uint256,addr.notice: Redeem HYBOND tokens with off-chain settlement in an arbitrary asset.

requestRedeem(address,uint256).notice: Queue a redeem request for T+2 pricing

revertRedeemToPending(uint256).notice: Revert redeems from final queue back to pending queue

setKycManager(address).notice: Rotate the KycManager. Requires DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE and all queues empty.

txsFee(uint256,uint8).notice: Calculate transaction fee based on amount and type

updateConvertRedeemRequestsDelay(uint256.notice: Update the redeem delay for T+2 processing

updateDepositMaxDeviationBps(uint256).notice: Update the symmetric oracle deviation tolerance for processDepositQueue

updateDepositMinimum(uint256).notice: Update deposit minimum amount (in token, 18 decimals)

updateEpoch().notice: Update epoch and accrue management fees based on offchainShares

updateFirstDeposit(address,bool).notice: Update first deposit status for an account

updateFirstDepositAmount(uint256).notice: Update first deposit requirement amount (in token, 18 decimals)

updateMaxStalePeriod(uint256).notice: Update the maximum stale period for price data

updateMgtFeeTo(address).notice: Update the management fee recipient address

updateOffchainShares(uint256).notice: Admin override for rare events (share splits, reconciliation).

updateRedeemMaxDeviationBps(uint256).notice: Update the symmetric oracle deviation tolerance for processPendingRedeems

updateRedeemMinimum(uint256).notice: Update redeem minimum amount (in token, 18 decimals)

updateTimeBuffer(uint256).notice: Update the minimum time buffer between epoch updates

updateTrimDecimals(uint8).notice: Update the number of decimals to keep when trimming amounts

updateTxFeeTo(address).notice: Update the transaction fee recipient address
